title.en: Biographies of Wei Bao and Peng Yue
title.raw: 《魏豹彭越列傳》 Biographies of Wei Bao and Peng Yue

sentences.zh: 魏豹者，故魏諸公子也。

translations.literal: Wei Bao was originally one of the various princes of Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Bao was a prince of the royal house of Wei.

sentences.zh: 其兄魏咎，故魏時封為寧陵君。

translations.literal: His elder brother Wei Jiu had in the old Wei times been enfeoffed as Lord of Ningling.
translations.idiomatic: His elder brother, Wei Jiu, had been enfeoffed as the Lord of Ningling during the days of the old Wei state.

sentences.zh: 秦滅魏，遷咎為家人。

translations.literal: When Qin destroyed Wei, it reduced Jiu to the status of a commoner.
translations.idiomatic: When Qin conquered Wei, Jiu was reduced to the status of a commoner.

sentences.zh: 陳勝之起王也，咎往從之。

translations.literal: When Chen Sheng rose up and proclaimed himself king, Jiu went to follow him.
translations.idiomatic: When Chen Sheng rose in rebellion and declared himself king, Jiu went to join him.

sentences.zh: 陳王使魏人周市徇魏地，魏地已下，欲相與立周市為魏王。

translations.literal: The King of Chen sent the Wei man Zhou Shi to make a circuit through the Wei territories; after the Wei territories had all submitted, the people wished together to set up Zhou Shi as King of Wei.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Chen sent Zhou Shi, a man of Wei, to pacify the old Wei territories. Once those lands had submitted, the people wished to make Zhou Shi the King of Wei.

sentences.zh: 周市曰：「天下昏亂，忠臣乃見。

translations.literal: Zhou Shi said: 'When all under heaven is in darkness and chaos, only then are loyal ministers revealed.'
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Shi said, "It is only when the world falls into darkness and chaos that loyal ministers make themselves known.

sentences.zh: 今天下共畔秦，其義必立魏王後乃可。」

translations.literal: Now all under heaven together rebel against Qin; by righteousness, one must first establish a descendant of the Wei kings, only then will it be permissible.
translations.idiomatic: Now all the world has risen against Qin. By any measure of righteousness, we must first restore a descendant of the Wei royal line to the throne."

sentences.zh: 齊、趙使車各五十乘，立周市為魏王。

translations.literal: Qi and Zhao each sent fifty chariots to establish Zhou Shi as King of Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Qi and Zhao each sent fifty chariots in support of installing Zhou Shi as King of Wei.

sentences.zh: 市辭不受，迎魏咎於陳。

translations.literal: Shi declined and did not accept, and went to welcome Wei Jiu from Chen.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou Shi refused the title and traveled to Chen to welcome Wei Jiu.

sentences.zh: 五反，陳王乃遣立咎為魏王。

translations.literal: After five round trips, the King of Chen then sent Jiu and established him as King of Wei.
translations.idiomatic: After five round trips of entreaty, the King of Chen finally released Jiu and established him as King of Wei.

sentences.zh: 章邯已破陳王，乃進兵擊魏王於臨濟。

translations.literal: After Zhang Han had defeated the King of Chen, he then advanced his troops to attack the King of Wei at Linji.
translations.idiomatic: After Zhang Han had defeated the King of Chen, he advanced his forces to attack the King of Wei at Linji.

sentences.zh: 魏王乃使周市出請救於齊、楚。

translations.literal: The King of Wei then sent Zhou Shi out to request aid from Qi and Chu.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Wei sent Zhou Shi to seek reinforcements from Qi and Chu.

sentences.zh: 齊、楚遣項它、田巴將兵隨市救魏。

translations.literal: Qi and Chu dispatched Xiang Ta and Tian Ba to lead troops following Shi to rescue Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Qi and Chu dispatched Xiang Ta and Tian Ba to lead troops in Zhou Shi's wake to relieve Wei.

sentences.zh: 章邯遂擊破殺周市等軍，圍臨濟。

translations.literal: Zhang Han then attacked and destroyed Zhou Shi and his army, and besieged Linji.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Han attacked and routed Zhou Shi's forces, killing Zhou Shi among others, and laid siege to Linji.

sentences.zh: 咎為其民約降。

translations.literal: Jiu, for the sake of his people, negotiated a surrender.
translations.idiomatic: Jiu negotiated a surrender to spare his people.

sentences.zh: 約定，咎自燒殺。

translations.literal: The terms were agreed upon, and Jiu set himself on fire and died.
translations.idiomatic: Once the terms of surrender were settled, Jiu immolated himself.

sentences.zh: 魏豹亡走楚。

translations.literal: Wei Bao fled to Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Bao fled to Chu.

sentences.zh: 楚懷王予魏豹數千人，復徇魏地。

translations.literal: King Huai of Chu gave Wei Bao several thousand men to again make a circuit through the Wei territories.
translations.idiomatic: King Huai of Chu gave Wei Bao several thousand troops to reclaim the Wei territories.

sentences.zh: 項羽已破秦，降章邯。

translations.literal: After Xiang Yu had defeated Qin and accepted the surrender of Zhang Han.
translations.idiomatic: After Xiang Yu had defeated Qin and accepted Zhang Han's surrender.

sentences.zh: 豹下魏二十餘城，立豹為魏王。

translations.literal: Bao took more than twenty cities in Wei, and Bao was established as King of Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Bao captured more than twenty cities in the Wei lands, and he was established as King of Wei.

sentences.zh: 豹引精兵從項羽入關。

translations.literal: Bao led elite troops to follow Xiang Yu into the Pass.
translations.idiomatic: Bao led his best troops to follow Xiang Yu through the passes into the heartland of Qin.

sentences.zh: 漢元年，項羽封諸侯，欲有梁地，乃徙魏王豹於河東，都平陽，為西魏王。

translations.literal: In the first year of Han, Xiang Yu enfeoffed the lords; wanting to possess the Liang territory himself, he moved King Bao of Wei to Hedong, with his capital at Pingyang, as King of Western Wei.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of the Han dynasty, when Xiang Yu parceled out domains among the lords, he coveted the Liang territory for himself and relocated King Bao of Wei to Hedong, with his capital at Pingyang, designating him King of Western Wei.

sentences.zh: 漢王還定三秦，渡臨晉，魏王豹以國屬焉，遂從擊楚於彭城。

translations.literal: When the King of Han returned to pacify the Three Qin, and crossed at Linjin, King Bao of Wei submitted his state to him, and then followed him to attack Chu at Pengcheng.
translations.idiomatic: When the King of Han returned to pacify the Three Qin and crossed the river at Linjin, King Bao of Wei submitted his kingdom and joined in the attack on Chu at Pengcheng.

sentences.zh: 漢敗，還至滎陽，豹請歸視親病，至國，即絕河津畔漢。

translations.literal: After the Han defeat, when they had returned to Xingyang, Bao requested leave to visit a sick relative; upon reaching his own state, he immediately cut off the river crossings and rebelled against Han.
translations.idiomatic: After the Han defeat, when they had retreated to Xingyang, Bao asked for leave to visit an ailing relative. Upon reaching his own domain, he promptly severed the river crossings and turned against Han.

sentences.zh: 漢王聞魏豹反，方東憂楚，未及擊，謂酈生曰：「緩頰往說魏豹，能下之，吾以萬戶封若。」

translations.literal: When the King of Han heard that Wei Bao had rebelled, he was preoccupied with Chu to the east and had no time to attack, so he said to Li Yiji: 'Go and use your eloquence to persuade Wei Bao; if you can make him submit, I will enfeoff you with ten thousand households.'
translations.idiomatic: When the King of Han learned of Wei Bao's rebellion, he was preoccupied with the threat from Chu to the east and could not spare forces for an attack. He said to Li Yiji, "Go and talk Wei Bao into submission. If you succeed, I shall grant you a fief of ten thousand households."

sentences.zh: 酈生說豹。

translations.literal: Li Yiji tried to persuade Bao.
translations.idiomatic: Li Yiji went to persuade Bao.

sentences.zh: 豹謝曰：「人生一世閒，如白駒過隙耳。

translations.literal: Bao declined, saying: 'A man's life in this world is like a white colt flashing past a crack in the wall.'
translations.idiomatic: Bao rebuffed him, saying, "A man's life in this world passes as swiftly as a white colt glimpsed through a crack in the wall.

sentences.zh: 今漢王慢而侮人，罵詈諸侯群臣如罵奴耳，非有上下禮節也，吾不忍復見也。」

translations.literal: Now the King of Han is arrogant and insults people, cursing the lords and ministers as if cursing slaves, with no regard for the proprieties between superior and subordinate; I cannot bear to see him again.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han is arrogant and abusive, cursing his lords and ministers as though they were slaves, with no regard for the proprieties between ruler and subject. I cannot bear to face him again."

sentences.zh: 於是漢王遣韓信擊虜豹於河東，傳詣滎陽，以豹國為郡。

translations.literal: Thereupon the King of Han dispatched Han Xin to attack and capture Bao in Hedong, had him transported under escort to Xingyang, and converted Bao's kingdom into a commandery.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han then dispatched Han Xin to attack and capture Bao in Hedong. Bao was escorted under guard to Xingyang, and his kingdom was converted into a commandery.

sentences.zh: 漢王令豹守滎陽。

translations.literal: The King of Han ordered Bao to guard Xingyang.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han assigned Bao to the defense of Xingyang.

sentences.zh: 楚圍之急，周苛遂殺魏豹。

translations.literal: When Chu besieged it urgently, Zhou Ke then killed Wei Bao.
translations.idiomatic: When Chu pressed the siege fiercely, Zhou Ke put Wei Bao to death.

sentences.zh: 彭越者，昌邑人也，字仲。

translations.literal: Peng Yue was a man of Changyi, with the courtesy name Zhong.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Yue was a native of Changyi, and his courtesy name was Zhong.

sentences.zh: 常漁鉅野澤中，為群盜。

translations.literal: He regularly fished in the marshes of Juye, and was a bandit leader.
translations.idiomatic: He often fished in the marshes of Juye and led a band of outlaws.

sentences.zh: 陳勝、項梁之起，少年或謂越曰：「諸豪桀相立畔秦，仲可以來，亦效之。」

translations.literal: When Chen Sheng and Xiang Liang rose in revolt, some young men said to Yue: 'The various heroes are establishing themselves and rebelling against Qin; Zhong, you could come and do the same.'
translations.idiomatic: When Chen Sheng and Xiang Liang rose in revolt, some of the young men said to Yue, "The heroes everywhere are setting themselves up and rebelling against Qin. You could join them and follow their example, Zhong."

sentences.zh: 彭越曰：「兩龍方鬬，且待之。」

translations.literal: Peng Yue said: 'Two dragons are just now fighting; wait for the outcome.'
translations.idiomatic: Peng Yue replied, "Two dragons are locked in battle. Let us wait and see how it turns out."

sentences.zh: 居歲餘，澤閒少年相聚百餘人，往從彭越，曰：「請仲為長。」

translations.literal: After more than a year, over a hundred young men from the marshes gathered together and went to follow Peng Yue, saying: 'We ask that Zhong be our leader.'
translations.idiomatic: After more than a year, over a hundred young men from the marshes banded together and went to Peng Yue, saying, "We ask that you be our leader, Zhong."

sentences.zh: 越謝曰：「臣不願與諸君。」

translations.literal: Yue declined, saying: 'I do not wish to join you gentlemen.'
translations.idiomatic: Yue declined, saying, "I have no wish to join you."

sentences.zh: 少年彊請，乃許。

translations.literal: The young men pressed their request, and he finally agreed.
translations.idiomatic: The young men persisted in their entreaties until he finally agreed.

sentences.zh: 與期旦日日出會，後期者斬。

translations.literal: He arranged with them to assemble at sunrise the next day; those who arrived late would be beheaded.
translations.idiomatic: He set a time for them to assemble at sunrise the following day, declaring that anyone who arrived late would be beheaded.

sentences.zh: 旦日日出，十餘人后，後者至日中。

translations.literal: The next day at sunrise, more than ten men were late, the last arriving at noon.
translations.idiomatic: At sunrise the next day, more than ten men arrived late, with the last straggler appearing at noon.

sentences.zh: 於是越謝曰：「臣老，諸君彊以為長。

translations.literal: Thereupon Yue said apologetically: 'I am old, and you gentlemen insisted on making me your leader.'
translations.idiomatic: Yue then addressed them: "I am getting old, and you insisted on making me your leader.

sentences.zh: 今期而多後，不可盡誅，誅最後者一人。」

translations.literal: Now many were late for the appointed time; I cannot execute them all, so I will execute the one who arrived last.
translations.idiomatic: Now many of you arrived late. I cannot execute all of you, so I shall execute the one who arrived last."

sentences.zh: 令校長斬之。

translations.literal: He ordered the squad leader to behead him.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered his adjutant to behead the man.

sentences.zh: 皆笑曰：「何至是？

translations.literal: They all laughed, saying: 'Why go so far?'
translations.idiomatic: They all laughed and said, "Surely there is no need for that?

sentences.zh: 請後不敢。」

translations.literal: We promise we will not dare to be late again.
translations.idiomatic: We swear we will never be late again."

sentences.zh: 於是越乃引一人斬之，設壇祭，乃令徒屬。

translations.literal: Thereupon Yue had the one man dragged out and beheaded, set up an altar and made a sacrifice, and then issued orders to his followers.
translations.idiomatic: Yue had the man dragged out and beheaded, set up an altar to make a sacrifice, and then issued orders to his followers.

sentences.zh: 徒屬皆大驚，畏越，莫敢仰視。

translations.literal: The followers were all greatly alarmed, feared Yue, and none dared to raise their eyes to look at him.
translations.idiomatic: His followers were all terrified. They feared Yue so deeply that none dared to look up at him.

sentences.zh: 乃行略地，收諸侯散卒，得千餘人。

translations.literal: He then went out to seize territory, gathering scattered soldiers of the various lords, and obtained more than a thousand men.
translations.idiomatic: He then set out to seize territory, rallying scattered soldiers from across the land until he had gathered more than a thousand men.

sentences.zh: 沛公之從碭北擊昌邑，彭越助之。

translations.literal: When the Lord of Pei marched north from Dang to attack Changyi, Peng Yue assisted him.
translations.idiomatic: When the Lord of Pei marched north from Dang to attack Changyi, Peng Yue came to his aid.

sentences.zh: 昌邑未下，沛公引兵西。

translations.literal: Changyi had not yet fallen, and the Lord of Pei led his troops westward.
translations.idiomatic: Before Changyi fell, the Lord of Pei withdrew his forces and headed west.

sentences.zh: 彭越亦將其眾居鉅野中，收魏散卒。

translations.literal: Peng Yue also led his followers to remain in the Juye marshes, collecting scattered Wei soldiers.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Yue likewise kept his men in the Juye marshes, gathering Wei soldiers who had been scattered.

sentences.zh: 項籍入關，王諸侯，還歸，彭越眾萬餘人毋所屬。

translations.literal: When Xiang Ji entered the Pass, made kings of the lords, and returned, Peng Yue's force of over ten thousand men had no one to whom they belonged.
translations.idiomatic: When Xiang Ji entered the passes, installed the lords as kings, and departed, Peng Yue's force of more than ten thousand men remained without allegiance to any lord.

sentences.zh: 漢元年秋，齊王田榮畔項王，（漢）乃使人賜彭越將軍印，使下濟陰以擊楚。

translations.literal: In the autumn of the first year of Han, King Tian Rong of Qi rebelled against the King of Xiang, and Han sent a man to bestow upon Peng Yue a general's seal, ordering him to take Jiyin to attack Chu.
translations.idiomatic: In the autumn of the first year of Han, when King Tian Rong of Qi rebelled against the King of Xiang, Han sent an envoy to bestow upon Peng Yue the seal of a general and ordered him to take Jiyin and attack Chu.

sentences.zh: 楚命蕭公角將兵擊越，越大破楚軍。

translations.literal: Chu ordered Lord Xiao Jiao to lead troops to attack Yue, but Yue greatly defeated the Chu army.
translations.idiomatic: Chu dispatched Lord Xiao Jiao to lead forces against Yue, but Yue routed the Chu army.

sentences.zh: 漢王二年春，與魏王豹及諸侯東擊楚，彭越將其兵三萬餘人歸漢於外黃。

translations.literal: In the spring of the second year of the King of Han, when he marched east with King Bao of Wei and the other lords to attack Chu, Peng Yue led his troops of over thirty thousand men to submit to Han at Waihuang.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of the King of Han's second year, when he marched east with King Bao of Wei and the other lords to attack Chu, Peng Yue led his thirty thousand troops to submit to Han at Waihuang.

sentences.zh: 漢王曰：「彭將軍收魏地得十餘城，欲急立魏後。

translations.literal: The King of Han said: 'General Peng has recovered the Wei territory and taken more than ten cities, and wishes urgently to establish a descendant of Wei.'
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han said, "General Peng has recovered the Wei territories and taken more than ten cities, and he is eager to see a descendant of Wei restored to the throne.

sentences.zh: 今西魏王豹亦魏王咎從弟也，真魏後。」

translations.literal: Now King Bao of Western Wei is also a cousin of King Jiu of Wei, a true descendant of Wei.
translations.idiomatic: King Bao of Western Wei is a cousin of King Jiu of Wei and a true heir of the Wei royal line."

sentences.zh: 乃拜彭越為魏相國，擅將其兵，略定梁地。

translations.literal: He then appointed Peng Yue as Chancellor of Wei, giving him full command of his own troops, to seize and pacify the Liang territory.
translations.idiomatic: He then appointed Peng Yue as the Chancellor of Wei, granting him sole command of his forces to conquer and pacify the Liang region.

sentences.zh: 漢王之敗彭城解而西也，彭越皆復亡其所下城，獨將其兵北居河上。

translations.literal: When the King of Han was defeated at Pengcheng and retreated westward, Peng Yue lost all the cities he had taken, and led his troops alone to the north to station them along the Yellow River.
translations.idiomatic: When the King of Han was defeated at Pengcheng and fell back to the west, Peng Yue lost all the cities he had captured and withdrew his forces northward to take up a position along the Yellow River.

sentences.zh: 漢王三年，彭越常往來為漢游兵，擊楚，絕其後糧於梁地。

translations.literal: In the third year of the King of Han, Peng Yue constantly went back and forth as a roving force for Han, striking at Chu and cutting off its supply lines in the Liang territory.
translations.idiomatic: In the King of Han's third year, Peng Yue ranged back and forth as a mobile force for Han, raiding Chu and severing its supply lines in the Liang region.

sentences.zh: 漢四年冬，項王與漢王相距滎陽，彭越攻下睢陽、外黃十七城。

translations.literal: In the winter of the fourth year of Han, when the King of Xiang and the King of Han were facing each other at Xingyang, Peng Yue attacked and took Suiyang, Waihuang, and seventeen other cities.
translations.idiomatic: In the winter of the King of Han's fourth year, while the King of Xiang and the King of Han were locked in a standoff at Xingyang, Peng Yue attacked and captured seventeen cities, including Suiyang and Waihuang.

sentences.zh: 項王聞之，乃使曹咎守成皋，自東收彭越所下城邑，皆復為楚。

translations.literal: When the King of Xiang heard of this, he ordered Cao Jiu to hold Chenggao, and went east himself to recover the cities Peng Yue had taken, all of which returned to Chu.
translations.idiomatic: When the King of Xiang learned of this, he left Cao Jiu to hold Chenggao and marched east in person to reclaim the cities Peng Yue had taken, restoring them all to Chu.

sentences.zh: 越將其兵北走穀城。

translations.literal: Yue led his troops north and withdrew to Gucheng.
translations.idiomatic: Yue led his forces northward and withdrew to Gucheng.

sentences.zh: 漢五年秋，項王之南走陽夏，彭越復下昌邑旁二十餘城，得穀十餘萬斛，以給漢王食。

translations.literal: In the autumn of the fifth year of Han, when the King of Xiang went south to Yangxia, Peng Yue again took more than twenty cities around Changyi, and captured over a hundred thousand bushels of grain to supply the King of Han with provisions.
translations.idiomatic: In the autumn of the fifth year of Han, when the King of Xiang retreated south to Yangxia, Peng Yue once again captured more than twenty cities around Changyi and seized over a hundred thousand bushels of grain to provision the King of Han.

sentences.zh: 漢王敗，使使召彭越并力擊楚。

translations.literal: The King of Han was defeated and sent a messenger to summon Peng Yue to combine forces and attack Chu.
translations.idiomatic: After the King of Han suffered a defeat, he sent a messenger to summon Peng Yue to join forces against Chu.

sentences.zh: 越曰：「魏地初定，尚畏楚，未可去。」

translations.literal: Yue said: 'The Wei territory has only just been pacified; we still fear Chu and cannot leave yet.'
translations.idiomatic: Yue replied, "The Wei territory has only just been pacified. We still fear Chu and cannot yet afford to leave."

sentences.zh: 漢王追楚，為項籍所敗碧陵。

translations.literal: The King of Han pursued Chu and was defeated by Xiang Ji at Guling.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han pursued Chu but was defeated by Xiang Ji at Guling.

sentences.zh: 乃謂留侯曰：「諸侯兵不從，為之柰何？」

translations.literal: He then said to the Marquis of Liu: 'The armies of the lords do not follow me; what should I do?'
translations.idiomatic: He said to the Marquis of Liu, "The armies of the other lords will not come to my aid. What should I do?"

sentences.zh: 留侯曰：「齊王信之立，非君王之意，信亦不自堅。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Liu said: 'The installation of the King of Qi, Xin, was not Your Majesty's intention, and Xin himself is also not secure in his position.'
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Liu replied, "The King of Qi, Han Xin, was installed without Your Majesty's approval, and he himself feels insecure in his position.

sentences.zh: 彭越本定梁地，功多，始君王以魏豹故，拜彭越為魏相國。

translations.literal: Peng Yue originally pacified the Liang territory with great merit; at first, Your Majesty on account of Wei Bao appointed Peng Yue as Chancellor of Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Peng Yue was the one who originally pacified the Liang territory and rendered great service, yet because of Wei Bao, Your Majesty initially appointed Peng Yue merely as Chancellor of Wei.

sentences.zh: 今豹死毋後，且越亦欲王，而君王不蚤定。

translations.literal: Now Bao is dead and has no heir; moreover, Yue also desires to be a king, yet Your Majesty has not settled the matter early.
translations.idiomatic: Now that Bao is dead and has left no heir, Yue naturally desires a kingship of his own, yet Your Majesty has not moved to settle the matter.

sentences.zh: 與此兩國約：即勝楚，睢陽以北至穀城，皆以王彭相國；

translations.literal: Make a pact with these two states: if we defeat Chu, all the territory from Suiyang north to Gucheng shall be given to make Chancellor Peng a king;
translations.idiomatic: Make a compact with these two men: once Chu is defeated, all the territory from Suiyang northward to Gucheng shall be granted to Chancellor Peng as his kingdom;

sentences.zh: 從陳以東傅海，與齊王信。

translations.literal: from Chen eastward to the sea shall be given to the King of Qi, Xin.
translations.idiomatic: and the territory from Chen eastward to the sea shall be given to the King of Qi, Han Xin.

sentences.zh: 齊王信家在楚，此其意欲復得故邑。

translations.literal: The King of Qi, Xin, has his family home in Chu; his desire is to recover his old homeland.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Qi's ancestral home is in Chu, and his heart's desire is to recover his old homeland.

sentences.zh: 君王能出捐此地許二人，二人今可致；

translations.literal: If Your Majesty can relinquish these territories and promise them to these two men, the two of them can be brought to your side;
translations.idiomatic: If Your Majesty can part with these territories and promise them to these two men, both can be won over;

sentences.zh: 即不能，事未可知也。」

translations.literal: If you cannot, then the outcome of things cannot be known.
translations.idiomatic: If you cannot bring yourself to do this, the outcome of the war remains uncertain."

sentences.zh: 於是漢王乃發使使彭越，如留侯策。

translations.literal: Thereupon the King of Han dispatched an envoy to Peng Yue, following the Marquis of Liu's plan.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han accordingly dispatched an envoy to Peng Yue, following the strategy of the Marquis of Liu.

sentences.zh: 使者至，彭越乃悉引兵會垓下，遂破楚。

translations.literal: When the envoy arrived, Peng Yue then led all his troops to assemble at Gaixia, and they defeated Chu.
translations.idiomatic: When the envoy arrived, Peng Yue marshaled all his forces and converged on Gaixia, where they defeated Chu.

sentences.zh: （五年）項籍已死。

translations.literal: In the fifth year, Xiang Ji was dead.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth year, Xiang Ji was dead.

sentences.zh: 春，立彭越為梁王，都定陶。

translations.literal: In the spring, Peng Yue was established as King of Liang, with his capital at Dingtao.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring, Peng Yue was installed as the King of Liang, with his capital at Dingtao.

sentences.zh: 六年，朝陳。

translations.literal: In the sixth year, he attended court at Chen.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year, he attended court at Chen.

sentences.zh: 九年，十年，皆來朝長安。

translations.literal: In the ninth year and the tenth year, he came to attend court at Chang'an in both years.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth and tenth years, he came to attend court at Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 十年秋，陳豨反代地，高帝自往擊，至邯鄲，徵兵梁王。

translations.literal: In the autumn of the tenth year, Chen Xi rebelled in the Dai region; the Supreme Emperor went in person to attack him, reached Handan, and summoned troops from the King of Liang.
translations.idiomatic: In the autumn of the tenth year, Chen Xi rebelled in the region of Dai. The Emperor himself marched to attack, and upon reaching Handan, he summoned the King of Liang to send troops.

sentences.zh: 梁王稱病，使將將兵詣邯鄲。

translations.literal: The King of Liang pleaded illness and sent a general to lead troops to Handan.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Liang pleaded illness and sent a general to lead his troops to Handan in his stead.

sentences.zh: 高帝怒，使人讓梁王。

translations.literal: The Supreme Emperor was angered and sent someone to rebuke the King of Liang.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor was furious and sent an envoy to rebuke the King of Liang.

sentences.zh: 梁王恐，欲自往謝。

translations.literal: The King of Liang was afraid and wished to go in person to apologize.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Liang grew fearful and wished to go in person to offer his apologies.

sentences.zh: 其將扈輒曰：「王始不往，見讓而往，往則為禽矣。

translations.literal: His general Hu Zhe said: 'Your Majesty did not go at first; now that you have been rebuked, if you go, you will be captured.'
translations.idiomatic: His general Hu Zhe said, "Your Majesty chose not to go at first. Now that you have been rebuked, if you go, you will be seized.

sentences.zh: 不如遂發兵反。」

translations.literal: It would be better to raise troops and rebel.
translations.idiomatic: It would be better to raise your army and rebel outright."

sentences.zh: 梁王不聽，稱病。

translations.literal: The King of Liang did not listen and continued to plead illness.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Liang refused to listen and continued to plead illness.

sentences.zh: 梁王怒其太仆，欲斬之。

translations.literal: The King of Liang was angry with his Grand Coachman and wished to behead him.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Liang grew enraged at his Grand Coachman and wanted to have him executed.

sentences.zh: 太仆亡走漢，告梁王與扈輒謀反。
於是上使使掩梁王，梁王不覺，捕梁王，囚之雒陽。
有司治反形己具，請論如法。
上赦以為庶人，傳處蜀青衣。
西至鄭，逢呂后從長安來，欲之雒陽，道見彭王。
彭王為呂后泣涕，自言無罪，願處故昌邑。
呂后許諾，與俱東至雒陽。
呂后白上曰：「彭王壯士，今徙之蜀，此自遺患，不如遂誅之。
妾謹與俱來。」
於是呂后乃令其舍人彭越復謀反。
廷尉王恬開奏請族之。
上乃可，遂夷越宗族，國除。
太史公曰：魏豹、彭越雖故賤，然已席卷千里，南面稱孤，喋血乘勝日有聞矣。
懷畔逆之意，及敗，不死而虜囚，身被刑戮，何哉？
中材已上且羞其行，況王者乎！
彼無異故，智略絕人，獨患無身耳。
得攝尺寸之柄，其雲蒸龍變，欲有所會其度，以故幽囚而不辭云。
